storyInfo: A herd of rampaging burdenbeasts stampedes towards the First Temple, scattering conversations and goodbyes.

attributes.content: Does a Leithanian thin-tailed grass have single leaves or compound leaves?
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: No idea.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: There are several methods for extracting red velvet mallow essence. Which one is most effective at retaining its soothing properties?
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: No idea.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: This boronia that you planted...
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: All I did was help you shovel the soil. I have no idea why you plant it, or how.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: You'll be tested on all of these things, Lydia.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: The Athopoulou family has venerated Flora the Hero for generations, conducting plant-related work such as those associated with perfumes, medicinal herbs, and plant breeding.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: The elders are very serious about this legacy. You'll have to participate in the traditional heir selection ceremony in a few years. You need to remember these things.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Everyone knows you're the heir to the Athopoulou family.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Uncle Steros can't stop talking about how proud he is of his daughter. He'll declare you the Flora of Athopoulou any day now.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: I know my father hasn't been fair towards you...
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: He's already shown as much kindness as possible by taking me in after my parents passed away. Besides, you know I have no interest in plants.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: But father has scheduled a test for next week. You'll be punished again if you fail...
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: I peeked into Uncle Steros's notebook. The thyme harvest has been disappointing. I expect he'll be going to Corinia soon, in order to find suppliers to meet the shortfall.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Corinia? He didn't say anything—
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Crouching in the garden again, Lena? ... I'm going on a week-long trip. Take care of yourself and Lydia while I'm away.
attributes.name: Steros

attributes.content: Wow...
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: See? There are certain things that I'm good at, just like you're good at plants and perfumes.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: *Pant* *pant* Help! Somebody! Anybody!
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Some skata tradition! Why did Athopoulou have to do the selection ceremony out here in the middle of nowhere?
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: The one who finds the rarest plant is the one favored by Flora? Ridiculous!
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Someone's hurt! We need a doctor... and emergency inhibitors!
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: You'll hurt your throat shouting like that, Lydia... carrying me is hard enough as it is, put me down...
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Well, you can help by throwing away that big heavy plant! There's no one more suited to being the heir to House Athopoulou, with or without it!
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: I know, it doesn't mean anything to me now.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: The Flora of Athopoulou cannot be Infected. Father will never accept it. I'll probably have to leave Athenius as soon as we get back.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: You don't need the approval of that bastard, if he's so cruel as to cast aside his own daughter.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: But the family needs an heir.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Write it down, Lydia. Boraginales, Boraginaceae, Cynoglossoideae, Myosotis...
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: What do you mean... don't tell me about it, Lena! I don't want to know!
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Listen to me.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: It's a common plant across Terra, but it's only known to grow around marshes, and it has never been found in Minos.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: It reminded me of you.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: You dislike the family tradition, and you have no interest in plants or herbs, as unlike Athopoulou as can be... but you're observant, and you're a good communicator. You'll make a good heir.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: That's your place. I don't want it...
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Let's make a bet, shall we? Just like when we were little.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Take care of this rare plant for me. If the elders have no issue with my condition, then I'll never make you come with me to that 'boring' garden ever again.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: But if father shows disgust at my wounds... you must claim to have found this 'big heavy plant'.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Don't worry, Lydia. No one will bully you anymore, not even my father, once you become the heir.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: With you, the Athopoulou family will be in good hands.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Lydia...
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Why did you ask me to come to this public garden? I don't think there's any need for us to reminisce about old times.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: If you have something to say, say it. I have a lot waiting for me to do.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: The exchange ceremony is about to begin. Besides verifying the guest list, making sure the ceremony goes smoothly, I must—
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: You must announce to Athenius that Xanthos and Lykeion have been found guilty of treason for the intentional theft and destruction of a Minoan national treasure?
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Not satisfied with turning from perfumer to doctor? Are you making another career change to defense attorney?
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: This is a critical juncture for the relationship between the two nations. I cannot allow an ill-planned, impetuous theft to destroy everything.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: A century of war didn't destroy Minos. Are you working for the interests of the people of Athenius, or to protect your own position as Astynomos?
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: A cliched moral dilemma...
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Of course I want to protect my position as Astynomos.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: How do you think Rhodes Island managed to establish an office in Athenius in such a short time?
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Priests buried in ancient tomes, the chaotic council, the convoluted voting process... you would have waited an entire year, if you followed the 'traditional process'.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Kereseira's First Temple and 'following the old ways'? What a joke.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: How could the place be ready for today's exchange ceremony, if the Sargonian girl hadn't intervened and pushed the final phase through with modern, effective project management?
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: So you judged everything with your scales, not caring that the weights you are using are real people?
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: Do you believe that everything must give way to 'people', then?
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: I'll do everything I can to find a solution, before things get to the point of no return. My colleagues and friends will walk together with me, no matter how treacherous the path. As for your question...
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: I'm a doctor, Lydia. It's the basis of everything I do.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: The ground beneath them trembles. The sound of howling approaches.
Dozens of burdenbeasts jump across fences and benches, stampeding through the public garden.
The herd steps upon the spacious grass, running towards the tall building in the distance, seemingly quite sure of their destination.
In the frenzied stampede, a young burdenbeast is drawn to something to the side.
Two solemn human figures reflect in its wet, curious eyes, but it quickly loses interest in them, catching up with the herd and leaving only drifting flower petals behind.
The scent of flowers surrounds the two Athopoulous.
Their keen sense of smell, honed by rigorous training from a young age, allows them to easily identify the damaged flowers, but not the true thoughts of their counterparts.
Neither of us could ever change the other's mind. It's been this way since we were little.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: But there's a question I always wanted to ask you.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: What makes you so 'great', Lena Athopoulou?
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: Lydia...?
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: You approached a girl that all the Athopoulous disliked, took her to your favorite garden, and taught her knowledge about plants that she had no interest in, all of it on your own.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: You gave her the plant that determined the family's heir, thought the family was in good hands, and left Athenius, again all of it on your own.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: You never cared about what I was good at, and it never occurred to you that I stayed with the Athopoulou family, not out of any sense of responsibility, or for the heir's position...
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: I didn't dislike the garden, Lena.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: But you chose not to keep it. You chose to take it back from the family after it fell on hard times, turning it into a public facility.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: That's our fundamental difference.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: You hate the name Athopoulou as much as I do, but you're always looking backwards, even trying to get it back, while I never look back.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: I know you asked me to come here for the sake of the two prisoners. Maybe you're trying to delay me, or maybe you have some other plan in mind.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: After I became the heir to the family, it took me another two years to cast the mantle aside. Only now do I have the chance to do what I really wanted to.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: As you said, though, no one bullied me during that time. I played along with your game for ten minutes. It's my final gift to you.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: I owe you nothing now, Lena.
attributes.name: Lydia

attributes.content: I see.
attributes.name: Lena

attributes.content: The grey-haired Vulpo gazes at Lena's departing figure. She had imagined their reunion before Lena returned to Athenius; afterwards, she had imagined their parting.
Never before had she been so acutely conscious that they were going to part ways forever.
The woman before her is no longer her foster sister who took care of her, the Lena who gently but insistently instilled botanical knowledge in her.

Lena is a Rhodes Island operator now. She has her own dreams and aspirations, her own home that she will go back to, her own family that loves and supports her. None of it has anything to do with Lydia.
Suddenly, a thought without any basis whatsoever comes to Lydia's mind—
Even if Lena remains in Athenius, and continues to work to develop an Oripathy treatment system, and continues to have dealings with the Astynomos' Office...
This will be the last time she sees Lena ever again.
Phew... we should be safe from the burdenbeasts here.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Where's Master Xanthos?
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: I think I saw him head towards the First Temple. To say goodbye to Kassandra, probably. We'll find a way to meet up with him, don't worry.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Why are there stampeding burdenbeasts on the street? Aren't you the Astynomos' Grammateús? Shouldn't you be finding out what's happening?
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: There are already peace officers heading that way. They'll take care of it.
attributes.name: Hekádemos
Hekádemos
attributes.content: This stuff's none of my business anymore—

attributes.name: Hekádemos
attributes.content: I resigned.
To rescue Master Xanthos and me from prison? That's not necessary. We—
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: You wanted to say you could have gotten out on your own, right? Well, it doesn't matter. I was planning to quit anyway.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: What exactly is your plan, Lykeion?
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: I don't know what you're talking about.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Please, you never passed your classical drama courses and it shows.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: You had the exact same expression on your face when you submitted your withdrawal from priest school after contracting Oripathy.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: It was the day before the debate contest, and we were practicing archery as usual.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: You were a much better archer than I was. I was pissed and intentionally fired a shot into the distance with your bow. The accident happened when you were looking for the arrow...
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: I carried you down the mountain and ran into Xanthos. He ferried us to the best Oripathy doctor available, but you got infected in the end.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: And so we missed our seventh debate contest that would have broken our 3-3 tie.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Is this really the right time to talk about this?
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Since when did Minoans need the 'right time' to debate? In the halls of Theros Temple, on the streets with drunkards and gamblers, everywhere is fair game.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: I thought you would only be down for a day or two, and we would be back in the competition in a couple of years. But you just ran away.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: We never decided the winner all these years. It's about time you gave me back this last debate, Lykeion.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: What's the topic?
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Let's say... whether an Infected can become a hero.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Hekádemos!
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: It's inappropriate.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Why? It's a topic you're intimately familiar with.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: 'Oripathy saps the heroic spirit.' It's conventional wisdom that we all take for granted. When a child questions it, he or she is quickly put down by the elders who cite various heroic legends.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: There are no Minoan heroes with Oripathy in the legends, no depictions of Originium crystals on the temple statues.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Therefore, you, a student who aspired to be a hero, gave up on becoming a priest before the rules had a chance to become an obstacle...
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Your every deed is representative of your position on this topic.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Don't bother trying to provoke me. We both know there's no chance I'll lose this debate.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: I thought about starting over, and Master Xanthos encouraged me. But I realized that someone who knows he has no future cannot commit himself to anything.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Regular suppressant injections, a regular lifestyle, and the newest Rhodes Island treatment may make the patient live longer.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: But I'm not satisfied with just living. Yet it seems to be the only thing I can do, and I feel nothing but shame about it.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: The worst thing about Oripathy is not the physical torment, but the inevitable deadline that it sets for all dreams and aspirations.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Even if Oripathy doesn't sap the heroic spirit, even if you could become a priest after becoming infected, even if the door to becoming a hero used to be open once—
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: That door is closed now. Do you understand?
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: I see.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: In that case... I win this last debate.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Who said the debate's over?
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: What?
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: You've demonstrated your position with your actions. I shall do the same, Lykeion. I will make you a hero of Minos.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Have you any idea what you're saying?
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: There's no other way to get you to admit defeat, is there?
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Xanthos could encourage you, or the deeds of other Infected could move you, but deep down, you would not admit that being infected or not has nothing to do with one's heroic spirit.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: In that case, the simplest way is to make a hero out of you.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: I've already quit my job. Wherever you go now, I'll follow, and do everything to make you keep on living, make you meet more people, make you be a part of important things.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: What is this, some newfangled punishment? What do YOU get out of it?
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: You know I was never the most motivated guy. I grew up to be that guy who always punches out exactly on time and never breaks a sweat when he could help it. Heroes are way too far away from my life, and I don't want to become like them, no matter how glorious it might seem.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: I'll make a hero out of you, a true hero, one whom everybody recognizes. As for what's in it for me—
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: I get to win our last debate. That's all I want.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: ...You're out of your mind.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Thanks for the compliment. I'll take that as a yes.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Oh, but you're late-stage, aren't you? That's a pretty big disadvantage for me... is it true that you have difficulty seeing and you get dizzy easily?
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: What are you talking about? I'm just mildly infected. I don't have those kinds of symptoms.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Mildly infected? But you keep going to the Rhodes Island clinic, Kyría Athopoulou had to pay you a home visit, and you rammed the hotel kitchen because you couldn't see the stop sign clearly.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: You've got it all wrong... it's Master Xanthos whose condition is serious.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Xanthos?
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: We never had a plan, Hekádemos.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Master Xanthos broke out of prison with me because he wanted to do something that was worthwhile to him, while he still could.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Even if the price is his life.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: You shouldn't have come here, Xanthos. People are gathering at the temple. You might have been able to avoid the crowd when you came, but it's going to be much harder to leave. Go, now.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: I came to say goodbye. I don't think we'll meet again.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Whether the farewell is face-to-face or not doesn't matter. What matters is that you're at peace. Go back to your homeland, Xanthos.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Go and see that old river. Even if it has dried up, the melted snow always flows down from the mountains. Maybe another water source nourishes your homeland now.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: I know. The priestess of Endymion watches over all our souls.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: You always give us false hope.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I remember the day you first came to Athenius. It was a rainy night, and you walked into my temple with Ithakos's bloody chiton.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: You were downtrodden and lost. But you told me about your promise with him when he went to war. You said you imagined yourself ferrying him to the temple on the other side of the city on the Delphi Canal.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: It was the flame of hope, faint and weak, but burning nonetheless. It is not a false hope that I instilled. It is a real hope, burning inside your heart.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: It was real.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: It still is, only that I had hoped you would nourish it with life, but it grew into something else as you ruminated over it.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Going back to your homeland, you might remember the past that was even longer ago, and let your pain be soothed by the voices of the past in the never-changing winds.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: What about the Sargonians?
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I know you think about the exchange ceremony. Divine revelation has already made arrangements.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: When the harp that belongs to Minos sings its song again, the people will once again be united, and Kereseira shall drive out the outlanders.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Divine revelation again.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: There was always a question that I wanted to ask.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: What IS divine revelation? How does it get people to willingly go to their deaths?
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: It's the power of faith and belief. It's the accumulation of a thousand years of dreaming about heroes, the thing that makes Minoans Minoan.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: We do not have 'gods', in the sense of the word that the outlanders use. Our gods are our heroes, each and every one of them.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Our hero Kereseira was once an ordinary woman.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: It was the guidance of divine revelation that led her to sing the songs of heroes and unite the suffering Minoans on the path that leads to where we are today.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Divine revelation represents your deepest desires. Believe in it, and it will guide your path and make you a hero.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Do you mean that Ithakos longed for death?
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Nine years ago, Ithakos came to the temple and asked me if his dream of a river running red with blood was divine revelation.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: I wanted to say no and tell him not to go, but I couldn't do it.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Divine revelation poured forth from my tongue, but I could not stop it or alter it, because it was not my place to deny someone else their most earnest desire.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: As for Ithakos...
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: The blood and tears shed in the construction of the nomadic city of Athenius run deep in his bloodline. It was ever in his mind even when we served the temple together.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: He sought a way out for his soul, a place beyond Athenius that he could build and protect.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: But death was the inevitable price for this wish.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Perhaps you're telling the truth, but I have a hard time accepting it.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: I know. Suffering rages in your blood, which pale words cannot soothe. Nothing I say can truly console you.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: But I wish you could—*sigh*
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: It's been five years since you came to Athenius, five years since we met.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: You spend most of your time at the river. You never come to the temple, and we rarely see each other except in front of Ithakos's grave.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: I never needed the guidance of a priest.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: But I was always watching you.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: You were Ithakos's friend, the last trace that his life left behind. I could find some small comfort if you lived well—it was one little bit of selfishness that I allowed myself, outside of my duties as priestess.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: I watched you acclimate to Athenius's rivers, watched you take in an apprentice, and watched the two of you go about your days together.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Lykeion is an honest, serious boy. It made my heart at ease that he was taking care of you.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Empty words could not comfort you. But what about these details of life? Have you not found comfort in it, even for an instant?
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Was Athenius everything you thought it would be? There must have been something that was better than you expected, wasn't there?
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: ...Maybe.
attributes.name: Xanthos

attributes.content: Then go think back to it, and think back to your past. And think about whether you've forgotten anything that was more important.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: You need to live well... even if only for Ithakos's sake.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: I hear something outside. I need to take a look.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: The hour is near, Xanthos. Leave quickly, and avoid the crowds.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: The temple's so big... how do I get to the audience seats of the theater from here...
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: That Corinian priest with a long name said he was 'going first'... or was it 'going away for a moment'? Is he coming back?
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: What do I do...?
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: What's that sound?
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: Moo—!
attributes.name: Frightened Burdenbeasts

attributes.content: Huh?
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: Celery! Onion! Eggplant! Wait for me!
attributes.name: Melitta

attributes.content: *pant* *pant*... Not a single burdenbeast responded to me the whole way from the ranch to the city... what's going on? Did something frighten you?
attributes.name: Melitta

attributes.content: Moo—
attributes.name: Small Burdenbeast

attributes.content: Tomato! Were you waiting for me at the rear of the herd? Guess the extra care I gave you was worth it!
attributes.name: Melitta

attributes.content: Just give me a moment to... catch my breath—
attributes.name: Melitta

attributes.content: Mmph!
attributes.name: Small Burdenbeast
Melitta
attributes.content: Oh, Tomato—

attributes.name: Melitta
attributes.content: Tomato! Come back! Wait for me!
Moo—
attributes.name: Massive Burdenbeast

attributes.content: There are a lot of people in the city. It's dangerous to run around like this.
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: Besides, it's rude to run and make noise in Kereseira's temple.
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: Moo.
attributes.name: Massive Burdenbeast

attributes.content: That's right. Good one.
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: The burdenbeasts... they've stopped. All she did was gently put her fingers on the burdenbeast's head. Even Cauliflower, who's normally the grumpiest...
attributes.name: Melitta

attributes.content: They say that even burdenbeasts could understand the music that Kereseira played on her harp. I always thought it was just a legend... convinced myself it must be...
attributes.name: Melitta

attributes.content: I never thought I would actually meet someone who could talk to burdenbeasts for real!
attributes.name: Melitta

attributes.content: Who... who are you? How do you talk to burdenbeasts? Can you teach me?
attributes.name: Melitta

attributes.content: Talk? All you need to do is open your mouth... you can do it, can't you?
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: No, what I mean is, how do the burdenbeasts understand what you're saying—
attributes.name: Melitta

attributes.content: They don't. At least, I don't think they do.
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: The girl lifts the finger that was on Cauliflower's forehead. The angry burdenbeast immediately lowers its shoulders and stomps its hooves, preparing to charge.
The girl puts the finger back.
You still have strength left. Impressive.
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: Are you the herder? Give me another minute. She'll settle down once she's out of energy.
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: The rest of the herd will calm down once the leader does.
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: So you just overpowered Cauliflower... with a single finger? So easily? It usually takes four or five strong guys to keep her down, and even that's not a guarantee...
attributes.name: Melitta

attributes.content: Your name's Cauliflower? Nice name. It suits you well.
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: *Whine*...
attributes.name: Massive Burdenbeast

attributes.content: Th-The tile beneath Cauliflower's feet is cracking!
attributes.name: Melitta

attributes.content: Alright, that should do it.
attributes.name: Stray Tourist

attributes.content: The girl lets go, and Cauliflower's knees buckle as she lies down on the ground.
Then the girl does it to the rest of Cauliflower's herd, one by one, calming all the burdenbeasts in no time.

attributes.name: Stray Tourist
attributes.content: That's the last one. Huh?

attributes.name: Stray Tourist
attributes.content: What's that on your horn?
Thank you for your assistance, our distinguished guest, Cynisca, honored priestess of Lachedamon.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: As for the harp you are holding, please give it to me.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: It is the harp of Kereseira, belonging to the First Temple, which we have been waiting for.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Titi! Found you at last!
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Did you follow the stampede of burdenbeasts to the temple? What happened at the ranch?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: I... I just fell asleep at the ranch last night. Then I heard a huge commotion before Mio woke me up, and I saw that the harp was gone...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I saw the burdenbeasts running down the hill, so I followed them and ended up here... was it the burdenbeasts that brought the harp back to the square?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Yes... it's the miracle that we've been waiting for! The true harp of Kereseira!
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Now, the only thing missing for the ceremony is the play...
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Which isn't so hard. I memorized the Kereseira roles in all the plays. Once we settle on a program, we can find people to play the rest of the roles...
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: About that—I would like to select the play for this ceremony, in my capacity as the supervisor of the First Temple's completion.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Why are you so serious all of a sudden? What's the play you have in mind?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Kereseira and the Outlander.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: You mean... the play that was banned for political reasons, and afterwards remained unperformed even after the ban was lifted because no Minoan wanted to play the role of the outlander?
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: Yes. It used to be the most popular classical play used in traditional ceremonies.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I will play the role of the outlander.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: You don't need to do this. We all know how kind-hearted you are...
attributes.name: Pallas

attributes.content: No. I want to do it.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I thought long and hard about it last night, and realized that this was my responsibility as a Sargonian, here and now.
attributes.name: Titi
